reasoning: Achieved occupancy is only 2.1%, which is far below the <35% threshold and is the clearest limiting factor for latency hiding. The kernel uses 64 KB of dynamic shared memory per block with only 32 threads per block, which severely restricts blocks per SM; meanwhile DRAM bandwidth utilization is just 0.3% of peak, L2 hit rate is 100%, and long scoreboard stalls are only 3.4%, so this is not bandwidth-bound or primarily latency-stalled on memory.
